steps.expected: Kanban board loads with 6 status columns and task cards
steps.observed: Board loaded with Backlog(4), Ready(1), Review(1), Done(1) columns populated

steps.action: Use agent-browser drag command to drag 'Drag test - backlog task' from Backlog column to Ready column
steps.expected: Task card moves to Ready column and API call updates task status
steps.observed: Task moved from Backlog to Ready column in the UI. Success toast appeared briefly.

steps.action: Verify task status via API: GET /api/tasks/47ea275e-2ea0-4259-8867-7e857489c6be

steps.expected: Task is still in Ready column after refresh
steps.observed: Task 'Drag test - backlog task' confirmed in Ready column (ref=e14) in snapshot

evidence.network: PATCH /api/tasks/47ea275e-2ea0-4259-8867-7e857489c6be with {status: 'ready'} -> 200 (inferred from UI behavior and API verification; network capture was not set up during this drag)
evidence.observations: Drag-and-drop works using agent-browser's built-in drag command. The PointerSensor activation constraint (distance: 5px) requires proper drag gesture. The agent-browser drag command handles this correctly. Task status persisted via API call and survived page refresh. Success toast appeared confirming the move to 'Ready'.

steps.action: Drag 'Restore task 2' from Backlog column to Done column using agent-browser drag command
steps.expected: Drop is prevented, error toast shown explaining valid transitions
steps.observed: Task remained in Backlog column. Red error toast appeared with message.

steps.action: Capture error toast text via DOM evaluation
steps.expected: Toast explains invalid transition and lists valid moves
steps.observed: Toast text: 'Cannot move from "Backlog" to "Done". Valid moves: Ready, Blocked'

steps.action: Verify task status via API: GET /api/tasks/4ef7ba70-31da-4e71-9420-c1523ace1257
Check network requests for PATCH calls
steps.expected: No PATCH request made (client-side validation prevented API call)
steps.observed: Only GET requests (polling) found - no PATCH requests for invalid drag

evidence.network: No PATCH requests made during invalid drag - only GET polling requests. Client-side validation in isValidStatusTransition() prevented the API call entirely.
evidence.observations: Invalid drag (backlog -> done) is properly prevented client-side. The KanbanBoard.handleDragEnd function checks isValidStatusTransition() and calls addToast() with getInvalidTransitionMessage() which returns: 'Cannot move from "Backlog" to "Done". Valid moves: Ready, Blocked'. The toast is red (error type), appears at top-right, has a dismiss button (×), and auto-dismisses after 4 seconds. No visual column highlight for invalid drops - the column border highlight (isOver state) is dnd-kit's native behavior but the validation happens in onDragEnd, not during drag-over.

frictions.description: Initial mouse-based drag (mouse move/down/move/up) did not trigger dnd-kit's PointerSensor. The PointerSensor requires a 5px activation distance before recognizing the drag. agent-browser's built-in 'drag @e11 @e13' command handles this correctly by performing a proper drag gesture.
frictions.resolution: Used agent-browser's built-in drag command (drag @source @target) instead of manual mouse operations. This works reliably with dnd-kit.

frictions.description: Toast messages auto-dismiss after 4 seconds, making it difficult to capture them in screenshots if there's a delay. Had to chain the drag command and screenshot/eval immediately to capture the toast.
frictions.resolution: Chained drag command with eval/screenshot in the same shell invocation to capture toast before 4-second auto-dismiss timeout.

frictions.description: Seeded data was mostly deleted by other parallel validators. Had to create test tasks via API (POST /api/tasks) and set up their statuses through valid transitions before testing.
frictions.resolution: Created tasks with unique prefix 'Drag test -' and moved them through valid status transitions (backlog->ready->in_progress->review->done) via PATCH API to set up test data.

summary: Tested 2 assertions: both PASSED. VAL-DASH-003: Drag-and-drop successfully moves tasks between columns, updates status via API, and change persists after refresh. VAL-DASH-014: Invalid drag (backlog->done) is properly prevented with clear error toast listing valid transitions, no API call made.
